name: pii-shield
description: Anonymize PII in documents. Zero-install: single drag-drop in Claude Desktop, bundled Node proxy bootstraps a Python venv on first run, includes the contract-analysis skill.
long_description: Single drag-drop install in Claude Desktop. MCP server that anonymizes documents before Claude sees them and restores real data after analysis — PII never enters the API, only file paths and session IDs are exchanged. v2.0.0 brings back the high-recall Python backend (presidio + GLiNER + spaCy) from v1.1.0 with zero manual setup: a Node proxy ships in the extension and, on first use, downloads python-build-standalone (or reuses your system Python ≥3.10), creates a venv, and installs the required packages. Subsequent launches are instant. Includes the `pii-contract-analyze` skill so contract anonymization works end-to-end out of the box. When you open a Cowork session on the same Claude Desktop, the MCP tools and skill are automatically inherited via the Desktop SDK pass-through, so anonymization runs host-side (real Python + GLiNER) and the HITL review opens in your host browser. Supports PDF, DOCX (with tracked changes), and plain text.

tools.name: find_file
tools.description: Find a file on the host by filename. Searches the configured working directory only.

tools.name: anonymize_file
tools.description: Anonymize PII in a file (.pdf, .docx, .txt, .md, .csv). PREFERRED — PII stays on host. Pass review_session_id for HITL re-anonymization (server applies overrides internally).

tools.name: deanonymize_text
tools.description: Restore PII to local .docx file (never returns PII to Claude)

tools.name: deanonymize_docx
tools.description: Restore PII in .docx preserving formatting (file only)

tools.name: get_mapping
tools.description: Get placeholder keys and types (no real PII values)

tools.name: list_entities
tools.description: Show status, supported types, and recent sessions

tools.name: start_review
tools.description: Start local review server and open the review page in the default browser. PII stays on your machine.

tools.name: get_review_status
tools.description: Check if user approved the HITL review. Returns status and has_changes only (no PII or override details).

tools.name: anonymize_next_chunk
tools.description: Process next chunk of a chunked anonymization session. Returns progress and partial result.

tools.name: get_full_anonymized_text
tools.description: Assemble all processed chunks and finalize the anonymization. Returns output_path and session_id.

tools.name: resolve_path
tools.description: Zero-config file path resolution. Finds a marker file on host via BFS to map VM paths to host paths.

gliner_model.title: GLiNER NER model
gliner_model.description: HuggingFace GLiNER model name for zero-shot NER

work_dir.title: Working directory
work_dir.description: Folder path where your documents are stored (e.g. C:\Users\You\Documents\contracts). Used by find_file and anonymize_file for automatic path resolution.

python_path.title: Python path (optional)
python_path.description: Full path to python3 binary. Leave empty for auto-detection.

skip_review.title: Skip HITL review
skip_review.description: Set to 'true' to skip the human review step after anonymization. By default, PII Shield opens a local review page where you can verify and correct anonymization before analysis.
